title: allium intermedium var. bulbiferum
intro: Allium intermedium var. bulbiferum, also known as the intermediate onion, is a perennial plant belonging to the Allium genus, characterized by its bulbous structure and onion-like flavor. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as respiratory problems, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Allium intermedium var. bulbiferum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. Herbal preparations of Allium intermedium var. bulbiferum can be made by drying the plant's leaves and flowers, then infusing them in hot water or oil to create a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
